title: Spec-Driven Development Deep Dive — Part 4: Building a Simple SDD Workflow Using GitHub Issues
description: No time to deploy a heavy enterprise-grade SDD framework? This article teaches you how to cleverly avoid memory decay in LLM sessions, and provides 5 practical steps to build a lightweight specification-driven workflow using native GitHub Issues and the GitHub CLI.
content:
Spec-Driven Development Deep Dive — Part 4: Building a Simple SDD Workflow Using GitHub Issues

How can I implement a lightweight SDD workflow using GitHub Issues?You can directly utilize the project's native GitHub Issues as a persistent external memory pool for large language models. This can be achieved by combining it with the GitHub CLI (…).ghThe team is able to effectively combat LLM cross-session context decay without introducing a heavy framework, achieving a zero-threshold, specification-driven development closed-loop experience.

TL;DRSmall teams too busy to build and learn complex enterprise-level architectures like Speckit? You can actually leverage the built-in GitHub Issues console and GitHub CLI in your project.ghIn almost a minute, a highly resilient SDD memory environment can be built, completely taming the cumbersome and lengthy large LLM session workflow that poses a risk of catastrophic forgetting.

Series Navigation:

  • Part 1: The Essence and Challenges of SDD
  • Part 2: GitHub Spec Kit Practical Guide
  • Part 3: Intent Layer Infrastructure and the Future of SDD
  • Part 4 (This Post): Building a Simple SDD Workflow Using GitHub Issues


Illustration: Leveraging the existing natural ticket ecosystem—transforming the Issue system into the most stable and flawless persistent memory cache stream for Agents.

Why choose GitHub Issues as the "external brain" of LLM?

We often face this situation: when performing large-scale functional analysis using tools like Gemini, Claude Code, or ChatGPT, as the conversation deepens, we inevitably encounter drastic...Memory decay (Context Decay)Symptoms. The lack of a systematic approach leads to the continuous loss of important basic environmental parameters or engineering structure assumptions in large language models, causing the originally efficient development experience to deteriorate into mechanical repetition.

Since you're already using a repository that includes a ready-made bug fixing system and source tracking tools, bringing in GitHub Issues as a persistent external anchor point for aligning agent intent with constraining facts becomes the most natural and lossless choice across the entire chain. This approach is beneficial for systems already in operation.CI/CD Automated BuildFor the codebase, the integration cost is zero.

The core advantage of this extremely simplified and lightweight architecture lies in:

  • native project cohesionAll your background information and the results data obtained after adjustments by the intelligent agent are kept closely together with the code in one place; refuse to scatter knowledge silos to messy third-party dialogue web service platforms.
  • Complete version control capabilitiesIt directly adds a special Agent positioning label to routine system maintenance, assigns clear node milestones, and allows for stacking, collapsing, and even permission-locked historical tree tracing flow.
  • Command-line highly scalable and user-friendly ecosystemNaturally supports native featuresgh(GitHub CLI tool) allows for highly programmable intervention, which enables extremely vast and powerful operations for anyone who writes a dozen lines of automatic batch processing hooks to intercept large models and obtain context.

Five-segment zero-pressure closed-loop current based on GitHub CLI

Imagine you are processing a program namedkeyYou have successfully logged into and associated the repository in your local environment. Now, let's introduce the Agent to solve a medium-complex task. The logical framework is as follows:

flowchart TD
    subgraph Local Environment
        A[1. 开发者在终端针对特定问题提示 LLM] --> B[LLM 拆解返回任务设计块与代码快照]
        B --> C[3. 实际本地编译与功能核验]
    end

    subgraph GitHub Issues System
        D[2. gh issue create: 推送需求并建立固化问题底座号]
        E[4. gh issue comment: 依编号追溯反馈 LLM 不断演进的重构历程差异代码]
        F[5. gh issue view: LLM 新次开局断点接续抽取重汇 JSON 上下文]
        G[6. gh issue close: 成功解围，强制提纯结构规范入固定库底]
    end
    
    A -.->|建立关联主轴任务追踪| D
    B -.->|反馈代码流同步入云存档| E
    F -.->|对抗遗忘主动回采前夕进度| A
    C -->|判定完成触发验收转入并 PR| G

1. Local launch with target intent interaction to extract requirements.

Everything can proceed as usual, opening the interface window like Gemini or the IDE sidebar to display a prompt: "We must now thoroughly clean up the project."src/main.pyTracing the deadlock issue back to its source. "Upon receiving the core framework and issue delimitation report, do not rush to execute it. Quickly cache it and convert it into a local Markdown file log backup (e.g.:session-log.md）。

2. "Record the memory anchor date" within the project workflow system.

Refuse to let crucial initial solutions fade from memory! Use a single command to force the newly generated report to be pulled into the GitHub support tracking area:

gh issue create \
  --title "LLM-Assisted Bug Fix in src/main.py" \
  --body-file session-log.md \
  --label "llm-task,bug" \
  --assignee "@me"

💡Tips and Tricks: Always remember to use the designated labels for standardized products, such as...llm-taskThis plays a crucial role in differentiating the team's perception of a clean workflow when separating functional panels where AI handles the trial-and-error process from those handled entirely by humans. You then receive a fixed serial number assigned by the system.#123。

3. Micro-step advancement of execution flow and trajectory tracking

During the long days of mission engagements, whenever a usable breakthrough in the refactored version was achieved or a conclusion that had been overturned was discovered, this command was decisively used to add and stack the changes:

gh issue comment #123 --body "LLM 更新方案变轨指正：发现并非单纯的等待死锁，由于早期遗留引用的挂载顺序存在冲突而导致。[核心补丁说明] "

The advantage of using this type of flow is that it transforms the originally chaotic, lengthy, repetitive model communication into a transparent operational checkpoint with strict time-based imprints and hedging mechanisms. This achieves extremely accurate simulation of enterprise-level SDD.tasks.mdThe checkmarks for each layer of accounts receivable in the table.

4. Enables instant activation of special effects upon next breakpoint restart.

When you get home from get off work or need to pick up the relevant code again after a month, simply type the aggregation statement in the terminal:

gh issue view #123 --json title,body,comments > issue-context.json

Before launching a new round of chat tasks, forcibly putting this high-density...issue-context.jsonFeeding requires the Agent to first scan and retrieve supplementary information. This is actually due to zero impedance, which gives large models extremely smooth and coherent unbiased long-term persistent record recovery capabilities across dates.

5. Issue Contract Settlement and Advanced Deep Web Refinement

After overcoming all difficulties and successfully submitting the Pull Request, the process finally came to fruition. Before completely closing the case, we extracted the highest-level anti-settlement design value for a final refinement:

gh issue close #123 --comment "Resolved via PR #456. LLM 核心探坑启示录：关于旧资源生命周期的统一监管强制要求。"

Such actions are essentially a way to quietly enrich the system's database with a high degree of refinement, integration, and collection of experience-based historical decision-making norms.

Three advanced tips for integrating agent architecture into existing libraries

Once you're familiar with the basic operations, you can use advanced automated scripts to create geek-level modifications and combinations, dramatically increasing efficiency:

进阶门径 配置说明与极客方案落地点 显著的效能斩获
Standardized memory of class division 短期计算（挂载于前端当前窗）；中台工作缓储记忆段（存入对应专属 Issue 尾端）；永久宗主长效记忆域（必须摘录提炼放入项目目录下的特殊准则档案库中用于规范大范围共识底线）。 最快时间内压缩与截停无营养的废话提示吞吐；彻底摒除 Agent 开头经常患有的盲自信发散与乱搭车架构等“神游脱线（Hallucinations）”表现。
Highly anti-slip interception water flow automation integration 设置针对本机的 Git Hook 挂载事件或是专属预处理 Makefile ，只要进入提交通道强制打断先向特定的底座 Issue 投递关键修正片段更新数据反馈端流转。 重塑人手高频介入步骤产生的乏味疲惫时间成本。极其适合中大型的并行协同修改体系。
Delineating the red line and limiting the scope of interrogation 不要在贪图畅快时随意全盘丢库给 Agent 进行无限的咀嚼反馈分析。在得出最终决策代码前设置硬核限定确认询问动作：“依据工单编号#XXX内设定的准入边界限制判定，此刻该决策符合范围吗？” 使得一切天花乱坠的提议死死被收束锁定聚焦向了当下的刚性技术任务不偏向。

The series concludes with:

In this in-depth SDD guide series, whether you're building a massive edifice from scratch using the heavyweight specification-driven tool GitHub Spec Kit, or you want to easily test simplified specification constraints on crowded and dilapidated historical beams using the naturally provided Issue tool for on-the-go checking; they are all essentially the same closed-loop system leading to the same goal.

Our ultimate goal remains unchanged: to pre-construct a standardized flow of intent for the elusive and unpredictable "machine intelligence will-thinking entity," ensuring it never strays off course or veers into an abyss. Even if, one day, the code for future system reconstruction no longer requires human input and is entirely handled by the system itself, the responsibility of firmly controlling the direction, determining the core landing point of intent, and setting all safety boundary speed limits—the "high-level navigator's mindset"—will forever belong exclusively to the top-level architects who sit at the keyboard and are truly responsible for the operation of this vast digital world.
